history.qwen_observation: SB 3.3 upgrade removed or failed to pull in com.sun.mail implementation and fastjson2, causing missing package/class compilation errors.
history.qwen_rationale: Insert a POM_PATCH step before the SB upgrade to explicitly add com.sun.mail:jakarta.mail (for com.sun.mail.* packages) and force fastjson2 to a recent version, ensuring the compile classpath is satisfied before and after the framework migration.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed because com.sun.mail:jakarta.mail:2.1.3 could not be resolved in the configured Maven repository.
history.qwen_rationale: Changing the jakarta.mail version to 2.1.2, which is widely available in Maven Central and typical Nexus mirrors, should resolve the dependency resolution error and allow the POM patch step to succeed.

history.qwen_observation: Dependency resolution failed for com.sun.mail:jakarta.mail:2.1.2 in the local Nexus repository during the initial POM patch step.
history.qwen_rationale: Switching the jakarta.mail version to 2.1.3 (the version managed by Spring Boot 3.3) should resolve the artifact availability issue in Nexus. Keeping the fastjson2 force_version ensures compatibility. Once dependencies resolve, the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade and subsequent Java 21 migration steps can proceed.

history.qwen_observation: Dependency resolution failed because com.sun.mail:jakarta.mail:2.1.3 is not available in the local Nexus repository, blocking the initial fix_missing_deps step.
history.qwen_rationale: Remove the manual dependency injection that triggers the Nexus resolution failure. Proceed directly with the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade, which will manage Jakarta EE dependencies via its BOM, followed by the standard Java 21 plugin and build upgrades. If specific Jakarta artifacts are still missing later, they can be addressed with available Nexus versions or alternative groups.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed after Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade due to missing com.sun.mail.* implementation classes and fastjson2.Feature symbol, indicating missing or mismanaged dependencies.
history.qwen_rationale: Injecting com.sun.mail:jakarta.mail and com.alibaba.fastjson2:fastjson2 via a POM patch before the Spring Boot upgrade ensures the required implementation classes are on the classpath, resolving the package-not-found and symbol errors that block the build.
